title: The City of Syracuse wants to sell a prime site worth millions. What’s taking so long?
summary: The City of Syracuse has delayed the sale of a prime 2+ acre property at 725 Harrison Street (a century-old school building) for over a year despite chronic budget deficits. Two main bidders—SUNY Upstate Medical University and California developer Ascent Property Co. (offering $10M + 400 market-rate apartments)—are in negotiations with no clear timeline. Political tension exists over tax-base recovery and housing development priorities.

political_signal.description: Hogan (mayoral candidate) questioning delays and Upstate's track record on property development; advocacy for private developer and tax-revenue generation; potential conflict-of-interest implications given Roth's campaign donations to Hogan.

processing_notes: Article contains unrelated content injection (Iran missile attack) suggesting editorial quality issue
Campaign donation connection ($6K from Roth to Hogan) warrants conflict-of-interest tracking
RFP originally issued March 2024; negotiations ongoing as of June 2025 (15+ month delay)
Three bidders identified: Upstate, Ascent, and at least one other undisclosed
Dollar amounts: $450M state capital approval (Upstate), $10M offer (Ascent), $50.4M project value, $380K annual property tax estimate, $6K in campaign donations
